title: High-stakes / exam-prep deck pathway (expert sign-off + "not advice")
context: Certain high-value deck subjects (medical licensing, bar exam, CPA, safety certifications) carry significant risk if cards contain errors: learners may make high-stakes decisions based on memorized incorrect information. Before any such deck is authored, the project needs a documented pathway covering credentialed-expert sign-off requirements, 'study aid, not advice' framing requirements, official open syllabus restrictions, and an escalation checklist. This pathway must be defined before any high-stakes deck work begins.
objective: Define the high-stakes and exam-prep deck pathway, including credentialed-expert sign-off requirements, 'study aid, not advice' framing, official open syllabus restrictions, and the escalation and review checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines which subject categories qualify as high-stakes (medical, legal, financial, safety, licensed-exam) and why.
Specifies the credentialed-expert sign-off requirements: who qualifies as an expert, how sign-off is documented, and where it is recorded in the deck JSON.
Mandates 'study aid, not advice' framing and specifies the exact disclaimer language to include in high-stakes decks.
Restricts high-stakes decks to official open syllabi only; specifies what counts as an official open syllabus.
Includes an escalation and review checklist that reviewers use before a high-stakes deck PR can be merged.
output: A versioned high-stakes deck pathway document (docs/high-stakes-pathway.md) covering expert sign-off, framing requirements, syllabus restrictions, and the escalation checklist, CC-BY-4.0.
